brand.name: Mile Hi City Pickleball
brand.intro: Nine voices. One court. Every week, fresh stories from the players shaping pickleball culture in Colorado.

thisWeek.title: court day grwm
thisWeek.brief: Hook (0-3s): camera at chest height on yourself, casual fit, one raised eyebrow · text on screen: 'court day grwm.' Josh Fawaz's 'Like a Prayer' Baccij Remix plays underneath from start to finish. Cut through your prep in quick beats: outfit change to court kit, bag packed, drive to Mile Hi, through the front doors. Final beat: grab a JOOLA paddle off the rack and turn to face the navy 'GAME ON' wall · no talking needed. 45-60 seconds. Sync your arrival moment to the audio build · TikTok's sound library has the remix, search 'Josh Fawaz Like a Prayer.' GRWM format works here because it makes showing up to the court feel like an event, not a chore.
thisWeek.trendSource: Josh Fawaz's 'Like a Prayer' Baccij Remix has 22M+ Spotify plays and is charting in Australia and the UK as TikTok's 2026 Summer Anthem. GRWM (get ready with me) is one of TikTok's highest-retention formats for sports and lifestyle creators, driving strong watch-through on prep-to-activity content. Source: Spotify chart data + NewEngen June 2026 trends report. First example URL is a real TikTok video from Josh Fawaz's account; second is a TikTok discover page.

thisWeek.title: 5 things beginners miss
thisWeek.brief: Hook (0-3s): tight face shot, one finger up, on-screen text: '5 things nobody told me before I played at Mile Hi.' No slow setup, go straight into the list. Tip 1: grab the JOOLA paddle off the rack, not just any paddle. Tip 2: stand at the kitchen line, not the baseline · that's where every rally is actually won. Tip 3: you WILL miss your first dink · Coach Jill says that's part of the process, not a sign you're bad. Tip 4: the navy 'GAME ON' wall means you're on Court 1, the best court in the house. Tip 5: everyone on the court is rooting for you. End tight on face, small smile: 'now you know.' 30-45 seconds, talk fast, one take. The list structure keeps viewers watching because they're counting down to the next number · don't let dead air break the momentum.
thisWeek.trendSource: Number-plus-promise format ('5 things I wish I knew about...') is one of TikTok's highest-retention structures for instructional and discovery content, consistently appearing in NapoleonCat's June 2026 trending formats report. Summer is peak recreational sports discovery season, making first-timer content well-timed for the Mile Hi beginner market. No specific trending audio required · use any energetic track from your FYP. Both example URLs are TikTok discover pages.

thisWeek.title: same energy different court
thisWeek.brief: Hook (0-3s): hold up your phone showing a World Cup 2026 score or goal clip, then immediately cut to yourself holding a JOOLA paddle on Court 1 at Mile Hi · same face, same posture, zero explanation. On-screen text: 'same energy.' Use a trending World Cup crowd chant or horn audio from the FYP (search 'World Cup 2026 crowd' in TikTok sounds). Cut three quick reaction moments: fist pump after a point won, frustrated head shake after a missed shot, team high-five with whoever is on the court. End on the navy 'GAME ON' wall, paddles raised, text overlay: 'mile hi : POV.' 30-45 seconds, no script needed · just bring your match-day face to the court. Film Tuesday through Thursday while the World Cup FYP wave is at peak volume.
thisWeek.trendSource: FIFA named TikTok its official preferred platform for the 2026 World Cup, which started June 11. TikTok and FIFA unveiled 30 Creator Correspondents, signaling algorithmic boosts for World Cup audio and reaction content all month. Sports crossover formats using World Cup crowd sounds are getting elevated FYP distribution this week. Sources: TikTok Newsroom + Yahoo Sports, June 2026. Both example URLs are TikTok discover pages · search 'World Cup 2026 crowd' in TikTok sounds to find the audio.

thisWeek.title: i knew it was pickleball
thisWeek.brief: Taylor Swift's 'I Knew It, I Knew You' from the Toy Story 5 soundtrack is the #1 single driving nostalgic origin-story content across TikTok this week. Hook (0-3s): hold up a childhood photo on your phone screen, or look directly into the camera with a small smile · on-screen text: 'I knew it was pickleball.' Cut to: walking into Mile Hi, grabbing a JOOLA paddle off the rack, one natural moment on the court (a good rally, a laugh after a missed shot, a high-five). Let the Taylor Swift bridge carry the last 10 seconds. End on the navy 'GAME ON' wall. 30-45 seconds, one take · genuine energy wins over polished every time for this format. Film Monday or Tuesday while Toy Story 5 premiere buzz builds toward June 19. Search 'I Knew It I Knew You Taylor Swift' in TikTok sounds to find the audio.
thisWeek.trendSource: Taylor Swift released 'I Knew It, I Knew You' on June 5 for Toy Story 5 and it hit #1 in the UK, breaking Spotify's single-day streaming record for a country song by a female artist (Official Charts, Deadline, Variety, June 2026). The song is driving a wave of nostalgic 'I always knew this was for me' origin-story formats on TikTok, peaking around the June 19 theatrical release. Both example URLs are TikTok discover pages · search 'I Knew It I Knew You Taylor Swift' in TikTok's sound library.

creators.name: Quinn Gordon
creators.vibe: 2 videos at root · IG/TikTok pending confirm

belleComments.intro: Good job, squad! Videos are looking strong this week. Real love for the music sync on the edits, the storytelling about coming into Mile Hi (showing the doors, the desk, the courts), and the focus on what the venue actually feels like. That's the squad voice landing. A few small brand things to lock in across all 9 of you so the grid feels like ONE roster instead of nine different accounts. These aren't call-outs · just patterns I noticed this week that we can all level up on.

rules.title: captions · same look every time
rules.body: When everyone uses a different font, the feed looks off. Let's all use white text with a black outline so the squad reads as one team. The full recipe lives in section 07 'before you post' at the bottom of this portal. Peek at it before you edit.

rules.title: pov format · keep it the same
rules.body: Always go with 'mile hi : POV' · logo, then colon, then POV in all caps. Same format every video. Makes us instantly recognizable when someone scrolls past.

rules.title: caption position · center it
rules.body: Drop your caption right in the middle. If you can't center, nudge it just above the middle. The bottom of the screen is where the TikTok UI sits, so text down there gets covered up and nobody reads it.

rules.title: always have a caption
rules.body: Every video gets text on screen. Yes, even the dance ones (especially the dance ones). The caption is what makes you searchable AND what holds viewers who aren't already into pickleball.

rules.title: use the mile hi joola paddle
rules.body: Grab a Mile Hi JOOLA paddle before you film. Your own paddle, a Monarch, a friend's, those don't read as Mile Hi when someone scrolls past. The brand paddle IS the brand signal.

rules.title: keep it in color
rules.body: Mile Hi is bright. The court is orange and navy, the vibe is energy. Please skip black-and-white filters and moody desaturation. Color always.

rules.title: music · keep the energy up
rules.body: Mile Hi is energy + community, so default to songs that lift the room. Exception · if a trend card at the top of the portal specifically calls for a slower or moody song (it'll happen sometimes), that's fine, follow the trend. Otherwise, if a song makes YOU feel sluggish, it'll make your viewer scroll.

rules.title: follow the trend cards at the top
rules.body: Sometimes a trend will feel a little weird and that's okay. We pick them on purpose · they're part of our strategy and the data shows what's going to work this week. Trust the cards even when they don't make sense in the moment.

belleComments.outro: For everyone just starting · you're doing GREAT. These are small tweaks, not rewrites. Pick the 2 easiest for you and lock them in this week. The squad that iterates wins.

images.label: image 1
images.caption: Wrong paddle (Monarch). Use the Mile Hi JOOLA paddle. The brand paddle IS the signal on the FYP.

images.label: image 2
images.caption: Caption parked at the bottom. Pull it to center or slightly above. Nobody reads the lower third.

images.label: image 3
images.caption: Caption blends into the background. Switch to white text with a black outline so it pops on any shot.

images.label: image 4
images.caption: Story shot with no caption. Every clip needs text on screen, even the establishing ones. Tell the viewer what they're looking at in 2 seconds.

images.label: image 5
images.caption: Dance shot with no paddles in hand AND the logo on the wall is mirrored (filmed front-facing without flipping back). Keep paddles in the frame on dance trends, and un-mirror your front-camera clips.

images.label: image 6
images.caption: POV format is off. Use 'mile hi : POV' (logo, then colon, then POV in all caps). Lock this format every time.

images.label: image 7
images.caption: Black-and-white / desaturated filter. Mile Hi is color + court + energy. No grayscale, no moody.

images.label: image 8
images.caption: POV without the colon + the logo prefix. Same fix as image 6 · 'mile hi : POV' is the format.

images.label: image 9
images.caption: Caption font doesn't match the squad recipe. Quicksand Bold lowercase + Anton caps for the punch word. Same across everyone, every video.

images.label: image 10
images.caption: Caption at the bottom AND overlapping the orange court line. Hard to read. Center the text or push it slightly above middle.

belleComments.comment: Organize your Drive folder, every time. RAW = everything straight off your phone (even the takes you don't love). EDITED = your final cut, ready to post. We pull from EDITED only · if it's not there, it doesn't get posted. Takes 30 seconds to drag, saves everyone hours.

When you film the dance or lip-sync trends, ALWAYS keep the paddle in your hands. Even mid-spin, mid-twirl, mid-beat-drop. The paddle is the brand signal that makes us instantly recognizable on the FYP. Without it, the video could be from any sport, so please keep it on you the whole time.

weeklyReport.summary: No TikTok posts have hit the feed yet, so the Drive production is the signal this week. Five of nine creators added new edits during the Jun 8 cycle, with the strongest output landing in the final days of the window. Four new trends are live: a GRWM court-day with Josh Fawaz's summer anthem, a five-tip beginner format, a World Cup energy crossover, and a Taylor Swift Toy Story 5 nostalgia piece.

topCreator.name: The squad
topCreator.reason: Two creators tied at 4 new clips for the week, with solid contributions from three others. No TikTok metrics yet to separate the pack, so the squad takes the recognition this week.

watchOuts: Two creators still don't have Drive folders set up. If that's you, ping Tricia this week so your clips can be counted and queued before the trends expire.
Several clips landed in the last 24 hours of the cycle. If that's you, aim to drop one edit midweek so there is time to review and coordinate before the weekend.
No TikTok posts have been logged yet. If your edited clips are ready to go, coordinate with Belle on timing and captioning before you hit publish.
weeklyReport.production: 33 edited videos across the squad this week. Top of production at 8, median at 4.
